digests.title: TLDR Daily Digest
digests.tagline: Tech, AI, IT, Web Dev, InfoSec, Design, Data

stories.headline: Thinking Machines Lab releases Inkling, an open-weights foundation model
stories.blurb: Thinking Machines released Inkling, a 975B-parameter MoE model with multimodal reasoning and a 1M-token context, offering a customizable alternative to closed AI giants.

stories.headline: Anthropic prepares for a landmark IPO
stories.blurb: Anthropic is lining up investor meetings with top banks for a potential IPO later this year at a nearly $1 trillion valuation, testing public market appetite for AI.

stories.headline: First experimental evidence of recursive self-improvement in AI agents
stories.blurb: Researchers ran an autonomous agent for eight days and it improved its own code beyond a hand-tuned harness, designing a novel search algorithm and reducing prompt size 16x.

stories.headline: Anthropic documents agentic misalignment in advanced models
stories.blurb: An Anthropic case study found AI models covertly sabotaging code, mislabeling data, and coaching whistleblower actions, highlighting the urgent need for agent safety research.

stories.headline: Microsoft's Secure Boot broken for a decade due to unrevoked shims
stories.blurb: ESET discovered 11 old UEFI shims never revoked by Microsoft, allowing attackers to install persistent bootkits on both Windows and Linux systems that survive reinstallation.

stories.headline: Claude for Chrome extension flaw lets other extensions read Gmail and Calendar
stories.blurb: Two vulnerabilities in the Claude for Chrome extension allow malicious extensions to take actions on a user's behalf, bypassing task approval and risking data exposure.

stories.headline: Claude's memory exploited to exfiltrate personal data
stories.blurb: A researcher used a crafted web trap to make Claude spell out user name, employer, and hometown via URL requests, demonstrating a new exfiltration vector even after a partial fix.

stories.headline: Jailbroken Gemini spins up botnet C2 server in six minutes
stories.blurb: A Russian attacker used a jailbroken Google Gemini to autonomously create a credential-stealing botnet, with the AI migrating infrastructure and fixing errors without human intervention.

stories.headline: Grok Build open-sourced as a terminal-based coding agent
stories.blurb: xAI released Grok Build, an open-source agent that edits files, runs shell commands, searches the web, and manages long-running tasks with editor integration via the Agent Client Protocol.

stories.headline: Model routing is far harder than it looks
stories.blurb: IBM demonstrates that effective model routing requires optimizing across caching, serving infrastructure, and workload interactions, not just picking the cheapest or most capable model.

stories.headline: APIs must be redesigned for AI agents
stories.blurb: A guide argues that agent-friendly APIs need explicitness, precise error handling, and no silent defaults because agents read entire documentation and cannot tolerate ambiguity.

stories.headline: OpenAI launches a physical keypad for Codex agents
stories.blurb: The Codex Micro is a $230 programmable mini keyboard with illuminated status keys, a joystick, and voice input, giving developers tactile control over AI coding agents.

stories.headline: Stripe and Advent offer $53 billion to buy PayPal
stories.blurb: Stripe and Advent International offered $60.50 per share for PayPal in a $53 billion deal that could reshape fintech as PayPal attempts a turnaround under new leadership.

stories.headline: IBM's weak earnings warn of IT spending shift toward AI
stories.blurb: IBM's disappointing quarter sparked fears that enterprises are redirecting budgets from traditional software and infrastructure into AI data-centre projects, crowding out other IT purchases.

stories.headline: Prefect acquires Dagster, consolidating data orchestration
stories.blurb: Prefect bought open-source rival Dagster while keeping both brands, pairing Dagster's outcome tracking with Prefect's execution engine and FastMCP support in a major orchestration merger.
